cashlessPlannedHappy.title: Cashless · planned admission · happy path
cashlessPlannedHappy.summary: Planned surgery at a network hospital. Cashless eligibility confirmed, pre-auth approved on the same day, claim settled in full a week after discharge.
cashlessPlannedHappy.teaches: The clean baseline: every party meets its SLA, every document is in order, every state transition takes the obvious path. Watch how the engine tracks who's responsible at each step.

cashlessEmergencyQuery.title: Cashless · emergency · TPA query → resolved
cashlessEmergencyQuery.summary: Emergency admission. The TPA had a question about the case — the hospital answered, and the claim continued to settlement.
cashlessEmergencyQuery.teaches: How the in-query state works: a regulated pause, a clearly-owned response window, and how the claim re-enters the main flow when resolved.

reimbursementDeductionGrievance.title: Reimbursement · deduction · grievance
reimbursementDeductionGrievance.summary: Reimbursement claim with a partial settlement after deductions. The patient files a grievance, which is acknowledged and resolved.
reimbursementDeductionGrievance.teaches: How the engine handles the harder paths: the reimbursement flow, the awaiting-patient-action affordance, the partial-settlement statement that names every deduction, and the grievance route as a regulated remedy.

cashlessRejectedSlaBreach.title: Cashless · pre-auth rejected · grievance escalation
cashlessRejectedSlaBreach.summary: The TPA missed the pre-authorisation response deadline, then rejected the claim. The patient files a grievance — when level 1 doesn't move it, they escalate.
cashlessRejectedSlaBreach.teaches: How an SLA breach is rendered — not as alarm-red but as 'the deadline passed → here is your recourse'. And how the grievance escalation ladder is part of the engine, not an afterthought.

pre-admission.headline: You've decided to seek treatment
pre-admission.explanation: Nothing has been filed yet. This is the moment before your hospital insurance journey begins.

intimated.headline: Your hospitalisation has been intimated to the TPA
intimated.explanation: You (or someone on your behalf) told the Third-Party Administrator that you're going to be admitted. The TPA will now wait for the hospital to confirm.

admission-advised.headline: The doctor has signed your admission advice
admission-advised.explanation: Your treating doctor has formally said you need to be admitted. The hospital can now check whether your insurance will cover the stay without you paying up front.

pre-auth-pending.headline: The hospital has filed the pre-authorisation request
pre-auth-pending.explanation: The hospital sent your case to the TPA, asking them to approve treatment up to a budget. The TPA must respond — this is a regulated deadline.

in-treatment-cashless.headline: Cashless treatment is approved — focus on getting better
in-treatment-cashless.explanation: Pre-authorisation is in place. The hospital can treat you and bill the TPA directly. You should not be asked to pay for covered care during this stay.

discharged.headline: You've been discharged. The hospital is finalising the bill.
discharged.explanation: Your treatment is complete. The hospital now totals the bill and sends it to the TPA. There is a regulated deadline for the TPA to settle.

in-adjudication.headline: The TPA is reviewing the final bill
in-adjudication.explanation: The discharge bill is with the TPA. They check it against policy terms — what's covered, what isn't — and decide what they will pay.

in-query.headline: The TPA has raised a question
in-query.explanation: The TPA needs clarification before they can decide on pre-authorisation. The hospital has been asked to respond — there is a regulated deadline. You don't need to do anything; you can focus on getting better.

settled.headline: Your claim is settled
settled.explanation: The TPA has paid the hospital directly under your cashless arrangement. There is nothing left for you to do.

in-treatment-reimbursement.headline: You are being treated. You'll claim reimbursement after discharge.
in-treatment-reimbursement.explanation: Your insurer didn't pre-approve cashless treatment for this admission, so you'll pay the hospital and submit a reimbursement claim later. Keep every receipt and discharge summary.

awaiting-patient-action.headline: The TPA needs information from you
awaiting-patient-action.explanation: There's a specific question from the TPA. The claim is paused until you respond. You can take your time — the regulated window for responses to insurer/TPA queries is generous — but the sooner you reply, the sooner this moves.

partially-settled.headline: Your claim is partially settled
partially-settled.explanation: The insurer has paid part of what you claimed, with deductions itemised below. If you disagree with any deduction, you have a right to escalate through the grievance route. Each level has a regulated response window.

rejected.headline: The insurer has rejected this claim
rejected.explanation: The insurer says no, for the reason shown below. You have a right to appeal through the grievance process — there's a regulated escalation ladder, and many cases that look closed at this point are resolved through it.
